progress.modelNote: every bar reserves its last 20% as a test & rework buffer (hatched) — it only fills once the stream is tested in production; build progress alone can never reach 100%
progress.scopeNote: scope expanded 2026-06-11 after the first full review (admin wiring, portal redesigns, localization, DB-integration tests, integrations & release workstreams). Expanded again 2026-06-13: a scale & concurrency audit added 17 findings as new scope — backend core and hardening are diluted accordingly. New productroadmap.md items added after the baseline keep lowering the affected workstream automatically until they're done.
progress.headline: money loop live in production · product layer has caught up — scale & concurrency hardening is now the main lift to launch

workstreams.label: backend core
workstreams.note: money loop + loan capture model (auth-hold → capture → release) live and prod-verified; reconciliation amount-matching and the scale-audit integrity gaps (double-booking, accrual-outside-txn, settlement TOCTOU) outstanding

workstreams.label: customer app
workstreams.note: Debts hub, redesigned Loans + Order-detail schedule, durable 'reported' state with Undo, SYP-native, Sentry-instrumented; Arabic/RTL and store builds still missing

workstreams.label: admin console
workstreams.note: wired to the live API — KYC queue, dashboard, customers & transactions, super-admin Platform Settings; full redesign and the remaining ops flows pending

workstreams.label: merchant portal
workstreams.note: QR quote + order flow works and is rebranded to the design system; redesign planned

workstreams.label: workers
workstreams.note: engagement outbox engine + admin notification rules live; late-fee & dunning logic not started

workstreams.label: localization
workstreams.note: SYP-native money & frontends shipped (single currency, no conversion); Arabic + RTL still required for a Damascus launch

workstreams.label: hardening
workstreams.note: 124 unit tests green; Sentry + root ErrorBoundary + boot-time env validation across all 3 apps; a scale & concurrency audit opened 17 findings (per-process rate limits, missing indexes, pagination, idempotency) — the main remaining lift

workstreams.label: integrations
workstreams.note: push + SMS notification outbox with deep-links live; real SMS/OTP provider and bank-statement import still pending

workstreams.label: release & store
workstreams.note: iOS build #6 in TestFlight, EAS Update (OTA) live, EAS-managed signing, auto-submit wired; Android build, store listings & review pending

release.note: Path from a working app to live in the stores. Externally gated — needs Apple Developer and Google Play accounts and passes external review.

dist cert + provisioning profile + ASC API key — now EAS-managed (moved from local 2026-06-16)
build #6 — OTA-capable (expo-updates, 'production' channel) with Sentry instrumentation

steps.label: TestFlight
steps.note: build #6 uploaded to App Store Connect via auto-submit — Apple processing; OTA live for JS changes

testAccess.warning: Pre-launch demo access — production holds test/seed data only. These credentials will be removed and the DEV_OTP bypass disabled before go-live (tracked on the roadmap). Don't enter real personal or payment data.

surfaces.name: customer app
surfaces.runNote: open in Expo Go (no hosted URL yet — store builds on roadmap)

decisions.title: One monthly bill
decisions.detail: all purchases roll into a single payment reference due at month-end — one transfer clears everything

decisions.title: Month-end due dates
decisions.detail: every installment lands on a calendar month-end, never odd mid-month dates

decisions.title: Plan belongs to the merchant
decisions.detail: stores choose 1 or 3 monthly payments — shoppers never pick a plan at checkout

decisions.title: Customers pay Qisto, never the store
decisions.detail: repayments go to Qisto by bank transfer and are reconciled by reference — merchants are settled separately

decisions.title: Zero interest, merchant-financed
decisions.detail: customers pay no interest or fees — Qisto earns a 7% merchant fee per sale

decisions.title: Credit ladder
decisions.detail: down payment shrinks as customers repay on time — 60% for new customers down to 25% for trusted ones

decisions.title: Loans captured, not just held
decisions.detail: a loan is authorized at checkout (hold), captured on delivery, and released if cancelled — the ledger only commits real, delivered debt

decisions.title: Single currency — SYP only
decisions.detail: every surface displays and transacts in Syrian pounds; USD survives only as an internal ledger unit, with no conversion anywhere users can see

shipped.text: SYP-native single-currency money — no conversion anywhere; every frontend displays and transacts in Syrian pounds
admin console wired to the live API — KYC queue, dashboard, customers & transactions, and a super-admin Platform Settings screen (was mostly mock data)
loan capture model live — auth-hold → capture → release, flag-gated and verified in production
push + SMS notification engine — admin rule set, a durable outbox, and deep-link-on-tap into the right screen
Sentry error monitoring (customer build #6) plus root ErrorBoundary and boot-time env validation across all 3 apps
Debts hub (This month / Future / Loans), redesigned Loans and Order-detail payment schedule, and a durable 'reported' state with Undo
photo-first signup with Claude Vision OCR — behind a default-OFF flag pending a PII/sanctions sign-off
iOS customer app live in TestFlight (build #6) + EAS Update (OTA) — design & logic changes ship to installed apps without an App Store release
admin console & merchant portal rebranded to the Qisto design system and switched to auto-deploy — every push to main ships both, at the same URLs
full repayment cycle live — customer reports a transfer, admin reconciles, ledger posts, credit restores, loan closes
consolidated monthly bill — one reference per customer per month across all purchases and merchants
month-end installment model — plans owned by merchants, schedules generated automatically
critical role-check vulnerability found by adversarial audit, fixed and verified in production the same day
double-entry accounting ledger with balanced-posting guarantees behind every money movement

now.label: gate dev login bypass to non-production
scale audit criticals — ledger double-book, accrual outside txn, per-process rate limiter
pick one settlement model, align docs with code

next.label: DB-backed integration tests — recon, settlement, idempotency
Arabic + RTL across the customer app (SYP done)
indexing, pagination & idempotency keys on money routes

qaReports.note: Deep audits live in the qisto repo qa-reports/ folder; open cases are GitHub issues.

reports.title: Full end-to-end repo audit
reports.verdict: Whole-repo audit across backend, apps, infra and docs — money loop and capture model sound; observability, reliability and the scale findings called out as the path to launch-readiness.

reports.title: Scale & concurrency audit
reports.verdict: 23 findings — 6 critical, 11 high — on concurrency, idempotency, indexing and rate-limiting under load. Tracked as issues #13–29 and added to the roadmap as new scope (the headline % reflects the added work).

reports.title: Money-math end-to-end audit
reports.verdict: Core math verified correct: live production scenarios across 3 merchant configs, 41-invariant checklist, 400k-case property tests, full ledger balance. 4 HIGH + 4 MED hardening cases opened (failure/race paths, not wrong math).

openCases.title: Concurrency/integrity criticals: accrual outside capture txn (#13), ledger double-book on retry (#14), per-process rate limiter (#15), full-ledger balance scans (#16)
Auth amplification: argon2 per OTP call with no IP cap (#26); DEV_OTP master-bypass + rate-limit inflation (#27)
Settlement approve/pay race — possible double merchant payout
Delivery accrual fire-and-forget — lost ledger postings on crash
Zero-amount guards: $0 loans never close; 0%-fee settlements wedge
Settlement TOCTOU, reconcile deadlock/idempotency, missing indexes, unpaginated lists, unsized pool, no money-route idempotency keys
